title: 2 people face charges after newborn found dead in wooded area near Halifax
full_text:
2 people face charges after newborn found dead in wooded area near Halifax
Police say charges include concealing body of a child, obstruction
Halifax Regional Police say the body of an infant believed to be a missing newborn has been found, and two people face charges in connection with the case.
Police were notified Friday night by hospital staff regarding a 23-year-old woman in life-threatening condition who appeared to have just given birth. The baby was not at the hospital with her.
The woman had been taken to the hospital earlier Friday after paramedics and firefighters responded to a residence following an emergency call regarding a woman in distress.
Halifax Regional Police Staff Sgt. Pierre Bourdages said investigators do not yet know where the baby was born.
Police and ground search and rescue workers spent the weekend searching the city's Clayton Park area for the newborn.
In a statement issued Sunday afternoon, police said new information redirected them to Prospect Road in Goodwood, just outside of Halifax. The baby's body was discovered there in a wooded area off Old Coach Road around 3:20 p.m. AT Sunday.
Bourdages said the cause of death will be determined Monday by the medical examiner, who will also officially confirm the infant's identity.
The mother remains in hospital in critical condition. Bourdages noted that her injuries are believed to be the result of giving birth, rather than an act of violence.
Four people were initially taken into custody Saturday, leading to charges against two individuals.
Specific charges
Sukhpreet Singh, 23, a relative of the woman, and his wife, Ramandeep Kaur, 26, are scheduled to appear in Halifax provincial court on Monday.
They face charges of concealing the body of a child, obstruction, and offering an indignity to a dead body.
